entry.title: Re: DOCUMENT_MAIN03 – Update QM Inspection Lot Status from DIR Classification (CV01N)
entry.content:
Here is the final CLASS CODE for reference:

class /RGS/CL_IM_S_MMA_DOC_MAIN03 definition
  public
  final
  create public .

public section.

  interfaces IF_BADI_INTERFACE .
  interfaces IF_EX_DOCUMENT_MAIN03 .

  types:
    BEGIN OF gty_mch1_key,
        matnr TYPE matnr,
        charg TYPE charg_d,
      END OF gty_mch1_key .

  data MS_KEY type GTY_MCH1_KEY .
  data MV_STATUS type J_STATUS .

  class-methods GET_SYSTEM_MESSAGE
    importing
      !ID_TYPE type SYMSGTY optional
    returning
      value(RS_RETURN) type BAPIRET2 .
protected section.

  methods GET_TARGET_STATUS
    importing
      !IS_DRAW type DRAW
    returning
      value(RV_STATUS) type J_STATUS .
  methods GET_INSPECTION_LOT
    importing
      !IT_DRAD type DMS_TBL_DB_DRAD
    returning
      value(RV_PRUEFLOS) type QPLOS .
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS /RGS/CL_IM_S_MMA_DOC_MAIN03 IMPLEMENTATION.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Protected Method /RGS/CL_IM_S_MMA_DOC_MAIN03->GET_INSPECTION_LOT
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] IT_DRAD                        TYPE        DMS_TBL_DB_DRAD
* | [<-()] RV_PRUEFLOS                    TYPE        QPLOS
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD get_inspection_lot.

*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* BAdI Definition      : DOCUMENT_MAIN03                               *
* BAdI Implementation  : /RGS/S_DOCUMENT_MAIN03_ENH                    *
* Method               : GET_INSPECTION_LOT                            *
* Class                : /RGS/CL_S_MMA_DOCUMENT_MAIN03                 *
* Design Spec          :                                               *
* Functional Spec      :                                               *
* Process              : Get Inspection Lot associated with a          *
*                        DMS Document                                  *
* History -------------------------------------------------------------*
* Date       | Change     | Contributor | Description                  *
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 07.07.2026 | 3200039293 | THAKRALR    | Initial version              *
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* Purpose:
* Determines the Inspection Lot associated with a DMS Document.
*
* Search Sequence:
* 1. Direct Inspection Lot Object Link (QALS)
* 2. Batch Object Link (MCH1/MCHA)
* 3. Material Object Link (MARA)
*
* Returns:
*   RV_PRUEFLOS - Inspection Lot Number
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*


    DATA: lv_matnr TYPE matnr,
          lv_charg TYPE charg_d.

    CONSTANTS:
      gc_qals TYPE dokob VALUE 'QALS',
      gc_mara TYPE dokob VALUE 'MARA',
      gc_mch1 TYPE dokob VALUE 'MCH1',
      gc_mcha TYPE dokob VALUE 'MCHA'.


*"--------1. Direct Inspection Lot Link-------------"*
    DATA(ls_drad) = VALUE drad( it_drad[ dokob = gc_qals ] OPTIONAL ).
    IF ls_drad IS NOT INITIAL.
      rv_prueflos = CONV qplos( ls_drad-objky ).
*   Remove leading spaces if any
      CONDENSE rv_prueflos NO-GAPS.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

*"--------2. Batch Link (MCH1 / MCHA)-------------"*
    ls_drad = VALUE #( it_drad[ dokob = gc_mch1 ] OPTIONAL ).
    IF ls_drad IS INITIAL.
      ls_drad = VALUE #( it_drad[ dokob = gc_mcha ] OPTIONAL ).
    ENDIF.

    IF ls_drad IS NOT INITIAL.
      ms_key = ls_drad-objky.

      lv_matnr = ms_key-matnr.
      lv_charg = ms_key-charg.

      SELECT SINGLE InspectionLot
        FROM /rgs/s_b_qals
        WHERE MaterialNumber = @LV_matnr
          AND BatchNumber    = @LV_charg
          INTO @rv_prueflos.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        RETURN.
      ENDIF.

    ENDIF.

*"--------3. Material Link-------------"*
    CLEAR ls_drad.
    ls_drad = VALUE #(
                  it_drad[ dokob = gc_mara ]
                  OPTIONAL ).


    IF ls_drad IS NOT INITIAL.
      lv_matnr = ls_drad-objky.

*   Fallback: take most recent inspection lot

      SELECT SINGLE InspectionLot
        FROM /rgs/s_b_qals
        WHERE MaterialNumber = @LV_matnr
        INTO @rv_prueflos.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        RETURN.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.

  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Static Public Method /RGS/CL_IM_S_MMA_DOC_MAIN03=>GET_SYSTEM_MESSAGE
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] ID_TYPE                        TYPE        SYMSGTY(optional)
* | [<-()] RS_RETURN                      TYPE        BAPIRET2
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD GET_SYSTEM_MESSAGE.

*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* BAdI Definition      : DOCUMENT_MAIN03                               *
* BAdI Implementation  : /RGS/S_DOCUMENT_MAIN03_ENH                    *
* Method               : GET_SYSTEM_MESSAGE                            *
* Class                : /RGS/CL_S_MMA_DOCUMENT_MAIN03                 *
* Design Spec          :                                               *
* Functional Spec      :                                               *
* Process              : Build BAPIRET2 message structure from the     *
*                        current system message                        *
* History -------------------------------------------------------------*
* Date       | Change     | Contributor | Description                  *
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 07.07.2026 | 3200039293 | THAKRALR    | Initial version              *
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* Purpose:
* Builds and returns a BAPIRET2 structure using the current SY-MSG*
* values. An optional message type can be supplied to override the
* current system message type.
*
* Returns:
*   RS_RETURN - Message structure of type BAPIRET2
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*

    IF id_type IS NOT INITIAL.
      CALL FUNCTION 'BALW_BAPIRETURN_GET2'
        EXPORTING
          type   = id_type
          cl     = sy-msgid
          number = sy-msgno
          par1   = sy-msgv1
          par2   = sy-msgv2
          par3   = sy-msgv3
          par4   = sy-msgv4
        IMPORTING
          return = rs_return.
    ELSE.
      CALL FUNCTION 'BALW_BAPIRETURN_GET2'
        EXPORTING
          type   = sy-msgty
          cl     = sy-msgid
          number = sy-msgno
          par1   = sy-msgv1
          par2   = sy-msgv2
          par3   = sy-msgv3
          par4   = sy-msgv4
        IMPORTING
          return = rs_return.
    ENDIF.

  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Protected Method /RGS/CL_IM_S_MMA_DOC_MAIN03->GET_TARGET_STATUS
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] IS_DRAW                        TYPE        DRAW
* | [<-()] RV_STATUS                      TYPE        J_STATUS
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD get_target_status.

*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* BAdI Definition      : DOCUMENT_MAIN03                               *
* BAdI Implementation  : /RGS/S_DOCUMENT_MAIN03_ENH                    *
* Method               : GET_TARGET_STATUS                             *
* Class                : /RGS/CL_S_MMA_DOCUMENT_MAIN03                 *
* Design Spec          :                                               *
* Functional Spec      :                                               *
* Process              : Determine target Inspection Lot status from   *
*                        DIR Classification values                     *
* History -------------------------------------------------------------*
* Date       | Change     | Contributor | Description                  *
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 07.07.2026 | 3200039293 | THAKRALR    | Initial version              *
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* Purpose:
* Determines the target Inspection Lot user status based on the
* Category and SubCategory values maintained in the DIR
* Classification.
*
* Status Mapping:
* 1. Category = 00160, SubCategory = 002001 (CoT) ->
*    User Status   : QCQA  Internal       : E0006
* 2. Category = 00160, SubCategory = 002002 (CfV) ->
*    User Status   : CERT  Internal       : E0008
*
* Returns:
*   RV_STATUS - Target Inspection Lot User Status
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*


    CONSTANTS:
      lc_char_category    TYPE atnam VALUE 'YDMS_CATEGORY',
      lc_char_subcategory TYPE atnam VALUE 'YDMS_SUBCATEGORY',

      lc_category_cert    TYPE atwrt VALUE '00160',

      lc_subcat_cot       TYPE atwrt VALUE '002001',  "Certificate of Testing
      lc_subcat_cfv       TYPE atwrt VALUE '002002',  "Certificate from Vendor

      lc_status_cot       TYPE j_status VALUE 'E0006',
      lc_status_cfv       TYPE j_status VALUE 'E0008'.

    DATA lt_rmclausp TYPE TABLE OF rmclausp.

    SELECT atnam, atinn
      FROM /rgs/s_b_cabn
      INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_cabn)
     WHERE atnam IN ( @LC_char_category ,
                      @LC_char_subcategory ).
    CHECK sy-subrc = 0.

    DATA(lv_atinn_category)    = VALUE cabn-atinn( lt_cabn[ atnam = lc_char_category ]-atinn ).
    DATA(lv_atinn_subcategory) = VALUE cabn-atinn( lt_cabn[ atnam = lc_char_subcategory ]-atinn ).

    CHECK lv_atinn_category IS NOT INITIAL AND
          lv_atinn_subcategory IS NOT INITIAL.


    " 'BAPI_OBJCL_GETDETAIL' Does not return values as we need buffer values - not the DB values
    " FM 'CTMS_DDB_HAS_VALUES_INTERNAL' gave no result
    " This FM only return values in BEFORE_SAVE method, not in AFTER_SAVE.
    CALL FUNCTION 'CLFM_GET_INTERNAL_TABLES'
      EXPORTING
        i_allausp    = abap_true
      TABLES
        exp_ausp_tab = lt_rmclausp.


    DATA(lv_objek) = CONV bapi1003_key-object( |{ is_draw-dokar }{ is_draw-doknr ALPHA = IN }{ is_draw-dokvr }{ is_draw-doktl }| ).

    DATA(ls_category)    = VALUE rmclausp( lt_rmclausp[ objek = lv_objek
                                                        atinn = lv_atinn_category ]
                                           OPTIONAL ).
    DATA(ls_subcategory) = VALUE rmclausp( lt_rmclausp[ objek = lv_objek
                                                        atinn = lv_atinn_subcategory ]
                                           OPTIONAL ).


    CHECK ls_category-atwrt = lc_category_cert.

    CASE ls_subcategory-atwrt.
      WHEN lc_subcat_cfv.  "'002002'.  Certificate from Vendor
        rv_status = lc_status_cfv.
      WHEN lc_subcat_cot.  " '002001'. " Certificate of Testing
        rv_status = lc_status_cot.
      WHEN OTHERS.
        CLEAR: rv_status  .
    ENDCASE.

  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Public Method /RGS/CL_IM_S_MMA_DOC_MAIN03->IF_EX_DOCUMENT_MAIN03~AFTER_SAVE
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] API_FLAG                       TYPE        C(optional)
* | [--->] TCODE                          TYPE        SYST-TCODE(optional)
* | [--->] DRAW                           TYPE        DRAW(optional)
* | [--->] DRAT                           TYPE        DMS_TBL_DB_DRAT(optional)
* | [--->] DRAD                           TYPE        DMS_TBL_DB_DRAD(optional)
* | [--->] DRAP                           TYPE        DMS_TBL_DRAP(optional)
* | [--->] DRAZ                           TYPE        DMS_TBL_DB_DRAZ(optional)
* | [--->] DOCFILES                       TYPE        DMS_TBL_FILE(optional)
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD if_ex_document_main03~after_save.

*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* BAdI Definition      : DOCUMENT_MAIN03                               *
* BAdI Implementation  : /RGS/S_DOCUMENT_MAIN03_ENH                    *
* Method               : AFTER_SAVE                                    *
* Class                : /RGS/CL_S_MMA_DOCUMENT_MAIN03                 *
* Design Spec          :                                               *
* Functional Spec      :                                               *
* Process              : Update linked Inspection Lot status after     *
*                        successful DIR save                           *
* History -------------------------------------------------------------*
* Date       | Change     | Contributor | Description                  *
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 07.07.2026 | 3200039293 | THAKRALR    | Initial version              *
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* Purpose:
* Updates the user status of the Inspection Lot associated with the
* DIR after successful save.
*
* Processing:
* 1. Determine linked Inspection Lot.
* 2. Trigger asynchronous Inspection Lot status update.
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
    IF mv_status IS NOT INITIAL.

      "Determine inspection lot
      DATA(lv_prueflos) = get_inspection_lot( it_drad = drad ).

      CHECK lv_prueflos IS NOT INITIAL.
      " Update Inspectio Lot User Status
      CALL FUNCTION '/RGS/S_MMA_DIR_IL_STATUS_UPD'
        IN UPDATE TASK "  STARTING NEW TASK 'DIRILSTAT'
        EXPORTING
          iv_prueflos = lv_prueflos
          iv_status   = mv_status.
    ENDIF.
    CLEAR:mv_status.

  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Public Method /RGS/CL_IM_S_MMA_DOC_MAIN03->IF_EX_DOCUMENT_MAIN03~BEFORE_SAVE
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] API_FLAG                       TYPE        C(optional)
* | [--->] TCODE                          TYPE        SYST-TCODE(optional)
* | [<-->] UPDATE_TASK                    TYPE        C(optional)
* | [<-->] DRAW                           TYPE        DRAW(optional)
* | [<-->] DRAT                           TYPE        DMS_TBL_DRAT(optional)
* | [<-->] DRAD                           TYPE        DMS_TBL_DRAD(optional)
* | [<-->] DRAP                           TYPE        DMS_TBL_DRAP(optional)
* | [<-->] DRAZ                           TYPE        DMS_TBL_DRAZ(optional)
* | [<-->] DOCFILES                       TYPE        DMS_TBL_FILE(optional)
* | [EXC!] CANCEL
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD if_ex_document_main03~before_save.


*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* BAdI Definition      : DOCUMENT_MAIN03                               *
* BAdI Implementation  : /RGS/S_DOCUMENT_MAIN03_ENH                    *
* Method               : BEFORE_SAVE                                   *
* Class                : /RGS/CL_S_MMA_DOCUMENT_MAIN03                 *
* Design Spec          :                                               *
* Functional Spec      :                                               *
* Process              : Determine target Inspection Lot status based  *
*                        on DIR Classification values                  *
* History -------------------------------------------------------------*
* Date       | Change     | Contributor | Description                  *
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 07.07.2026 | 3200039293 | THAKRALR    | Initial version              *
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* Purpose:
* Determines the target Inspection Lot user status based on the
* Category and SubCategory maintained in the DIR Classification.
*
* Applicable DIR Types:
* - YQ3 : DIA Release Certificate
* - Y57 : Production & Process Controls
*
* Returns:
*   MV_STATUS - Target Inspection Lot User Status
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*

    CONSTANTS:
      lc_doc_type_yq3 TYPE dokar VALUE 'YQ3',
      lc_doc_type_y57 TYPE dokar VALUE 'Y57'.
    CLEAR:mv_status.
    " Process only supported DIR types
    CHECK draw-dokar = lc_doc_type_yq3 OR
          draw-dokar = lc_doc_type_y57.


    mv_status = get_target_status( is_draw = draw ).
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Public Method /RGS/CL_IM_S_MMA_DOC_MAIN03->IF_EX_DOCUMENT_MAIN03~DELETE_DOCUMENT
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [<-->] DOKAR                          TYPE        DRAW-DOKAR
* | [<-->] DOKNR                          TYPE        DRAW-DOKNR
* | [<-->] DOKTL                          TYPE        DRAW-DOKTL
* | [<-->] DOKVR                          TYPE        DRAW-DOKVR
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD if_ex_document_main03~delete_document.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Update FM:

FUNCTION /rgs/s_mma_dir_il_status_upd .
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Update Function Module:
*"
*"*"Local Interface:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     VALUE(IV_PRUEFLOS) TYPE  QPLOS
*"     VALUE(IV_STATUS) TYPE  J_STATUS
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
  DATA: lt_return	TYPE bapiret2_t  ##NEEDED,
        ls_return TYPE bapiret2    ##NEEDED.

  SELECT SINGLE ObjectNo           " Read Inspection Lot Object Number
    INTO @DATA(lv_objnr)
    FROM /rgs/s_b_qals
   WHERE inspectionlot = @iv_prueflos.

  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.
  CALL FUNCTION 'STATUS_CHANGE_EXTERN'
    EXPORTING
      objnr               = lv_objnr
      user_status         = iv_status
    EXCEPTIONS
      object_not_found    = 1
      status_inconsistent = 2
      status_not_allowed  = 3
      OTHERS              = 4.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    CASE sy-subrc.
      WHEN 1.
*   Object number & does not exist
        MESSAGE e601(bs) WITH lv_objnr INTO ls_return-message.
      WHEN 2.
*   UPDATE_JEST:STATUS_INCONSISTENT: OBJNR: & STATUS: &
        MESSAGE e701(bs) WITH lv_objnr iv_status.
      WHEN 3.
*   Status & is not allowed (&)
        MESSAGE e042(bs) WITH iv_status INTO ls_return-message.
      WHEN OTHERS.
        CLEAR: ls_return-message.
    ENDCASE.
    APPEND /rgs/cl_im_s_mma_doc_main03=>get_system_message( ) TO lt_return.

  ENDIF.

ENDFUNCTION.

entry.title: Re: GL Account name language issue in FBL1N line item
entry.content:
In the standard, transaction FBL1N does not display the G/L account name.

If you want the name to appear according to the logon language, this requires a customer-specific enhancement.
You can implement your own logic using one of the following extension points:
- BTE 00001650 
- BAdI FI_ITEMS_CH_DATA.

With either option, the logic should derive the correct language from the G/L account master record based on the logon language.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Account Architecture Best Practices: A Practitioner's Field Guide
entry.content:
Hi @ltomazeli58 

Good question, this comes up often when a team needs parallel release cadences for the same application.

Recommendation: Don't create a full duplicate subaccount tree per lane. Keep a single DEV subaccount and a single QA subaccount, and split each one internally into two Cloud Foundry spaces (or Kyma namespaces) - one per lane:

- DEV subaccount → space support-dev + space nextgen-dev
- QA subaccount → space support-qa + space nextgen-qa
- PROD remains a single subaccount for the application. Both lanes deploy into it on their own cadence (monthly for support, quarterly for the next major version), governed through your CI/CD pipeline (e.g., SAP Cloud Transport Management or the BTP CI/CD service) rather than through separate production accounts.

Why this works well for cost:

Subaccounts are the layer that actually costs you, each one requires its own slice of entitlement quota, its own Cloud Connector configuration (in hybrid landscapes), its own identity provider trust setup, and ongoing admin overhead. Spaces/namespaces, by contrast, are essentially free to create and tear down. They still give each lane full isolation - separate app deployments, separate service instances, separate users/roles, so your support team can test and promote on their monthly cycle without any interference from whatever is being built for the next major release in parallel. You get the isolation you need for simultaneous dev/test, while sharing the expensive parts (entitlement pool, connectivity, identity trust) across both lanes.

When a separate subaccount is actually justified:

a) The two lanes require incompatible service plans or versions that can't coexist under one shared entitlement pool
b) They need different identity providers or trust boundaries
c) Compliance or data-residency rules require hard separation
d) The 'next major version' lane is expected to run far longer than a typical release cycle, making the lighter-weight teardown of a space less relevant

One practical tip: Apply labels to your spaces and service instances (e.g., lane: support, lane: nextgen) so your consumption/cost dashboards can still attribute spend per lane even though the underlying subaccount is shared.

Hope it helps.

 

Regards,

Reddy

entry.title: From BTP to BAIP: SAP's Platform Rename Is a Much Bigger Architectural Signal Than It Looks
entry.content:
Effective June 30, 2026, SAP BTP Competency becomes SAP Business AI Platform (BAIP) Competency. Here's what it really means for partners, Solution Architects, and the ecosystem.

Jun 30

Effective Date

Auto

All BTP Partners Transitioned

Oct 26

New Specialization Structures

Jan 27

New Proficiency Requirements

A name change on a partner competency certificate might sound like an administrative footnote. But when SAP renames SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) to SAP Business AI Platform (BAIP), effective June 30, 2026, it's worth pausing to understand what that signal actually means.

This is a deliberate and strategic shift — not a rebrand. It reflects where SAP is heading: AI is no longer an add-on to the platform. It is the platform. And for every Solution Architect, SI, and ISV working in the SAP ecosystem, the conversation is changing.

"The platform is no longer just an enabler of integration, extension, and data management — it is the engagement and execution layer for AI-powered enterprises."  — SAP Partner Portal Announcement

 

  1.   What Has Actually Changed (And What Hasn't)

Let's be precise about what this update does and does not change, effective June 30, 2026:

 

What Changed

What Didn't Change

Competency name

BTP → BAIP — label only at this stage

Partner listing visibility

Updated automatically across SAP PartnerEdge & Partner Finder

No partner action needed

All existing BTP partners auto-transitioned to BAIP

Competency requirements

Unchanged at this stage — no new exams or certifications yet

Ongoing pursuits

All competency pursuits continue unchanged

 

The operational continuity is intentional. SAP has ensured zero disruption to existing partners. But the strategic direction it signals is significant — and the more substantive changes come later.

 

💡  Key Implications for SAP Partners & Solution Architects

→  No immediate action required — your competency status, tiers, and specializations are unchanged

→  Your customer-facing Partner Finder listing now shows BAIP — update your own marketing materials to match

→  Don't treat this as just an admin update — use it as a moment to reposition your platform narrative

 

  1.   Why This Change Matters — The Strategic Signal

The previous 'Business Technology Platform' label no longer fully captured three fundamental shifts happening inside SAP's portfolio:

 

Three Reasons the Rename Reflects a Real Shift

1.  AI is now central to SAP's innovation stack — not a layer on top of it. Every SAP application, process, and industry solution has Business AI embedded at its core.

2.  BTP, Business Data Cloud, and SAP Business AI have converged into a single platform (SAP Business AI Platform). This is the direct SAP Sapphire 2026 effect.

3.  The platform's role has expanded from integration + extension to enabling autonomous, intelligent business processes — Joule, agents, and agentic workflows running on top of it.

 

For Solution Architects, this is the shift that matters most. The conversation is moving:

 

Integration & Extension   ➡️   AI Agents · Autonomous Workflows · AI-Native Applications

 

The platform foundation remains the same. But where architectural effort and intellectual property will live — that is changing fast.

 

💡  Key Implications for SAP Partners & Solution Architects

→  Platform expertise alone will no longer differentiate you — demonstrable AI capability is the new bar

→  Customers will ask: 'Can you embed AI into our business processes?' — not just 'Can you integrate our systems?'

→  Building AI-ready business processes will become as important as building scalable integrations

 

  1.   Three Strategic Implications for Partners

Implication 1 — Partner Differentiation Will Be Evaluated Through an AI Lens

The BAIP competency should not be positioned as a rebranded credential. It needs to be positioned as evidence of readiness to deliver measurable, AI-driven business outcomes. Customers will increasingly evaluate partner capability not by what certifications they hold, but by what AI outcomes they have delivered.

 

  • Outcome-based AI case studies become the primary GTM asset — not credential lists
  • AI governance and agent lifecycle management skills become differentiating competencies
  • Partners who can demonstrate ROI from Joule Assistants and autonomous workflows will win the positioning battle

 

Implication 2 — This Is a Portfolio Expansion Inflection Point

The rename creates a clear window to expand offerings into new high-value areas aligned to where SAP is investing. Partners should begin aligning toward:

 

New Service Area

Why It Matters Now

AI-driven process automation

Joule Assistants across Finance, HCM, CX, Supply Chain — all need deployment expertise

Data-to-AI pipelines

SAP Knowledge Graph, BDC, Dremio — new data engineering and governance opportunities

Agentic use cases on SAP

Joule Studio (GA Q3 2026) — 680+ partner agents already submitted for TechEd

Intelligent app extensions

AI services embedded into custom applications built on BAIP

Clean Core AI assessments

Every ECC customer needs certification — repeatable, billable service

 

Implication 3 — GTM Narratives Must Evolve

This is perhaps the most urgent change. Partner go-to-market materials, sales decks, and customer conversations need to reflect the new positioning immediately — even before the competency requirements change.

 

From → To: GTM Narrative Shift

FROM: 'We hold the SAP BTP competency'

TO:   'We are certified to deliver AI-led transformation on SAP's Business AI Platform'

 

FROM: 'We build integrations and extensions on BTP'

TO:   'We design and deploy autonomous workflows and AI agents on BAIP'

 

FROM: 'We implement SAP solutions'

TO:   'We deliver measurable AI outcomes — measured by days-to-close, fill rate, time-to-hire'

 

  1.   What Comes Next — The Phased Framework Evolution

This rename is step one in a structured evolution of the BAIP competency framework. SAP has outlined specific milestones:

 

When

What's Coming

Now (Jun 30)

BTP competency renamed to BAIP — automatic for all existing partners, no action required

Oct 2026

Introduction of updated specialization structures and revised product coverage aligned to the AI-first portfolio

Jan 2027

Activation of new knowledge and proficiency requirements — with safeguarding periods to support partner transition

Ongoing

New enablement programmes, certifications, and GTM support as framework matures

 

The January 2027 milestone is the one to watch. That is when the competency framework will explicitly define the skills, certifications, and delivery capabilities expected of BAIP-qualified partners. Partners who begin upskilling now will have a significant head start.

 

💡  Key Implications for SAP Partners & Solution Architects

→  October 2026 is less than 4 months away — start scoping your portfolio alignment now

→  January 2027 requirements are not yet published — engage your PDM and SAP enablement team for early signals

→  Partners who have deployed production Joule Studio agents before Jan 2027 will be best positioned for the new requirements

 

  1.   Your Action Plan — What to Do This Month

Don't wait for October 2026 to start moving. The window to build first-mover advantage is open now. Here are the recommended actions, in priority order:

 

Action 1 — Reposition Your BTP Offerings as AI-Driven

Audit your current service catalogue — identify which offerings can be reframed as AI-led

Update sales decks, proposals, and partner listings to reflect BAIP language and AI outcomes

Train your presales and sales teams on the BAIP narrative this quarter

 

Action 2 — Invest in AI & Agent Competency

Register for Joule Studio Early Adopter Care — get ahead of the Q3 2026 GA

Upskill architects and developers in AI agent design, multi-step reasoning workflows, and governance

Build internal capability in data engineering and AI pipeline management on BDC

 

Action 3 — Build Outcome-Based Case Studies

Identify 2–3 existing customers where you can add Joule or AI capabilities and document outcomes

Frame results in business KPIs: days-to-close reduced, time-to-hire improved, cash flow accuracy

These case studies become your primary BAIP differentiation assets when Oct 2026 changes land

 

Action 4 — Align Internal Roadmaps to 2027 Requirements

Map your current competency portfolio against expected AI-first requirements

Engage your PDM now for advance guidance on what the Jan 2027 framework will require

Participate in SAP's Hack2Build sprints and partner enablement workshops as they are announced

 

The Bottom Line

"SAP BTP evolving into SAP Business AI Platform is more than a competency rename. It reflects where SAP is heading: AI is no longer an add-on — it becomes part of the platform itself."

A small name change. A much bigger architectural direction.

 

The partners who recognise this early — who reposition their capabilities, narratives, and investments now — will be the ones customers call when they are ready to operate autonomously. The ones who treat it as an admin update will find themselves in a catch-up position when October 2026 and January 2027 arrive.

If you are working in the SAP ecosystem as a Solution Architect, SI, or ISV, this is worth acting on — not just reading about.

Sources: Official SAP Partner Portal Announcement · SAP Community · June 30, 2026 · Views expressed are personal.

entry.title: Re: 🐍 July Developer Challenge Week 2 — Roll & Move
entry.content:
Your srv/game-service.js handler implementations (paste as a JS code block)

const cds = require('@SAP/cds')

module.exports = class GameService extends cds.ApplicationService {
  async init() {

    const { Players, BoardSquares } = this.entities

    // ── startTurn ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Waiting]  →  CAP validates status before this runs
    // @TO: #Playing      →  CAP updates status after this returns
    // No business logic needed — CAP's @flow.status handles the transition.
    this.on('startTurn', 'Players', async (req) => {
      // Intentionally empty: the @from/@to annotations do all the work.
    })

    // ── rollDice ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Playing]  →  CAP validates status before this runs
    // @TO: #Moving       →  CAP updates status after this returns
    this.on('rollDice', 'Players', async (req) => {
      const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID })
      if (!player) return req.error(404, 'Player not found')

      const roll = Math.ceil(Math.random() * 6)
      let newPosition = player.position + roll

      // Can't overshoot square 100 — must land exactly
      if (newPosition > 100) {
        await UPDATE(Players).set({ lastRoll: roll }).where({ ID: player.ID })
        const needed = 100 - player.position
        return req.reject(409, `Need exactly ${needed} to finish. Rolled ${roll}. No move.`)
      }

      // Resolve snake or ladder on the new square
      const square = await SELECT.one.from(BoardSquares).where({ square: newPosition })
      let finalPosition = newPosition
      let event = null

      if (square?.ladderTo) {
        finalPosition = square.ladderTo
        event = { type: 'ladder', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
      } else if (square?.snakeTo) {
        finalPosition = square.snakeTo
        event = square.isDoubleHead
          ? { type: 'doubleSnake', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
          : { type: 'snake', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
      }

      // Persist position and last roll
      await UPDATE(Players)
        .set({ position: finalPosition, lastRoll: roll })
        .where({ ID: player.ID })

      // Emit domain event so subscribers (Week 3) can react
      if (event) await this.emit('BoardEvent', { playerID: player.ID, ...event })

      // Use req.reply() — not return — to pin the response value before CAP's
      // @flow.status after-handler runs. A plain `return` gets cleared by the
      // flow machinery, producing HTTP 204 instead of the expected 200 + body.
      req.reply({ roll, position: finalPosition, event: event?.type ?? 'normal' })
    })

    // ── confirmMove ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving]  →  normal end of a turn, hands off to next player (Week 3)
    // @TO: #Waiting     →  player waits for their next turn
    this.on('confirmMove', 'Players', async (req) => {
      // Week 3 will add turn rotation here.
      // For now: just let CAP apply @TO: #Waiting
    })

    // ── blockPlayer ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving]  →  called when player lands on a double-headed snake
    // @TO: #Blocked     →  player skips their next turn
    this.on('blockPlayer', 'Players', async (req) => {
      // Business logic: the position was already updated in rollDice.
      // This action just transitions state — CAP handles the @TO: #Blocked update.
      req.info('Player is blocked and will skip their next turn.')
    })

    // ── unblockPlayer ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Blocked]   →  called at the start of a blocked player's next turn
    // @TO: $flow.previous →  CAP restores the status to whatever it was before #Blocked
    this.on('unblockPlayer', 'Players', async (req) => {
      // CAP's $flow.previous does the work — no code needed here.
      // The player's status returns to #Waiting automatically.
    })

    // ── winGame ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving]  →  called when player.position === 100
    // @TO: #Finished    →  player has won
    this.on('winGame', 'Players', async (req) => {
      const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID })
      if (!player) return req.error(404, 'Player not found')

      // Mark the session as finished with this player as winner
      await UPDATE('snakeladder.GameSessions')
        .set({
          finishedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
          winner_ID: player.ID
        })
        .where({ ID: player.session_ID })

      await this.emit('GameWon', { playerID: player.ID, sessionID: player.session_ID })
    })

    return super.init()
  }
}
 
waiting status after confirmMove

entry.title: Badi not found for check validation for batch with respect to storage location
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

We have the following business requirement in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud:

During Production Order Confirmation for an SFG material, the user enters both a storage location and a batch number for the produced material.

Business rule:

  • Storage Location SOR1 should only allow batches starting with SOR (e.g., SOR1001).
  • Storage Location SIL1 should only allow batches starting with SIL (e.g., SIL1001).

Example:

  • Storage Location = SOR1

  • Batch = SOR1001 → Allowed

  • Storage Location = SOR1

  • Batch = SIL1001 → System should issue an error message and prevent saving the confirmation.

We are looking for a suitable BAdI  that can be triggered during Production Order Confirmation to validate the combination of storage location and batch number before the confirmation is saved.

Could you please advise which released BAdI can be used for this requirement in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

Regards, 
Name-Sagar Nagose

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC EDI / IDOC Support
entry.content:
Hi Maurice,

you may want to listed to the DRC Germany webinars here under events: https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/document-reporting-compliance

EN 16931 is the required format, how it is exchanged in many ways is left up to sender/receiver. 

DRC does not support EDI transactions today as SAP's current focus is on current and nextGen technology.

entry.title: Re: RFC adapter in Integration Suite receives data from SAP backend but drops payload
entry.content:
For anyone that might go through this issue, upon further investigation, I found out that there were special characters coming from the backend SAP system (e.g., incorrectly encoded characters in unicode, which resulted in a 'square' being in the string of a specific field of the record being exposed in the RFC exporting table parameter). Therefore, from this record onwards, upon deserialization when receiving the XML payload back from the RFC, it throws a silent exception, dropping the content.

The fix is to guarantee that there are no special, incorrectly encoded characters in any text field in the data in the backend. this solved the issue for me.

 

thanks,

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | Restriction Field: User Group
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I create a role for the administrator of company 5140 to manage users belonging to company 5140.
Setup:

  • Business Role ID: ZBRS_5140_ADMINISTRATOR
  • The values ​​of Access Categories are Restricted.
  • The values of User Group field value are ZCB5140_USER. User Group is Leading Restriction.
  • Other field values are blank.

I think the User Group field supports displaying or changing user data.

The administrator with the role ZBRS_5140_ADMINISTRATOR can do the following (based on what I've tried):

  • View all user data on the system (even if those users are not in User Group ZCB5140_USER)

 

  • Edit user data belonging to User Group ZCB5140_USER
  • Cannot edit user data belonging to other User Groups

Question:

  • Why can this administrator view all user data on the system (even if those users are not in User Group ZCB5140_USER)
  • Is my role setup correct?

Thank you very much.

QuocDT5

entry.title: Re: Sap ewm: Error during mapping: material does not exist
entry.content:
If the error "Error during mapping: material XYZ does not exist"
Then check the entries in the table "/SCWM/TMAPSTLOC"
If the mapping is not done correctly for the logical system and the Sloc, Follow the below path
SPRO – Extended Warehouse Management - Interfaces - ERP Integration – Goods Movements - Map Storage Locations from ERP System to EWM

entry.title: Re: Processing Electronic Invoices in France with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content: @AdilHoussa  as you can see from your screenshot, the e-doc is created belong the wrong process 'France EU eInvoice'. If you want to submit to PPF and Peppol through PA process, the e-doc needs to be created under 'Domestic customer invoice'. You must correct/add missing configurations and create new e-documents. I'm afraid you have the incorrect setup and/or incorrect master data (for B2B invoices, you need to maintain valid SIREN/SIRET and remove the customer from EDOEUBUPAV). Please review the documentation and you might need some consulting support if you are struggling with the setup itself. This is not a product issue, but rather incorrect setup in your system.
